text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, young Olive wandered through the leaves. She loved these ventures! Today was especially enchanting; every corner she explored whispered secrets. After hours lost in fun adventures, chilly bones.

Olive found an old oak tree. Its leaves were a tapestry of browns. "It's October." She glanced around. Leaves rustled their greetings. "Like me?"

The owl hooting high seemed to think so.

They sat side by side. Olive felt cozy.

Owl spoke, in hoots rich as chestnuts. "Many find autumn boring. You prefer?"

"A book." Olive nestled deeper.

Owl chuckled softly.

Hours melted into evening.

Stars blinked shyly.

Olive stood quietly.

Owl spoke, gently. "There's nothing fancy about you."

Or maybe that's just why you love me.

Olive smiled.

They flew homeward.

That's how her favorite day began.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
